name: Bhai Gurpartap Singh (Hazoor Sahib)
body: Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji was born on 16th October 1978 in Patiala to Mata Kamaljeet Kaur Ji and Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji. His father, along with his uncle, Bhai Joginder Singh Ji were legendary figures in the field of music, especially in Kirtan. Music has been in their family for generations as they belong to the Rababi tradition of music dating back a few hundred years. His grandfather, Bhai Tarlochan Singh Mastana served as Hazoori Raagi in Gurdwara Janam Asthan Nankana Sahib prior to the partition of Punjab in 1947. After that, they settled in Patiala along with three other Rababi families from Nankana Sahib; Bhai Gurmukh Singh & Sarmukh Singh Fakkar, Bhai Jaswant Singh & Pal Singh and Bhai Mehar Singh Taan.

In 1976, Bhai Joginder Singh and Bhai Mohinder Singh accompanied ‘Jathedar’ Baba Santa Singh Ji of 96 Crori Panth Akali Budha Dal to Sachkhand Sri Hazoor Sahib, Nanded. During this visit, the Sangat of Nanded were so mesmerized by their Kirtan that they were requested to stay on. Since then, their family has been serving Sachkhand Sri Hazoor Sahib.

Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji’s training in music began at home under the guidance of his father and uncle. He also learned from Pandit Shyamrao Gunjkar, one of the finest disciples of Pandit Jasraj Ji. Pandit Shyamrao Gunjkar was a good family friend of theirs and was considered as a younger brother by Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji. Due to their close relationship, he used to frequent their house and spend hours teaching Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji and his siblings. Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji is the third out of five children. His eldest brother is the late Bhai Gurpreet Singh Ji followed by Bhai Harminder Singh Ji, Bhai Tejmaninder Singh Ji and Bhai Maninder Singh Ji.

Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji has been trained in tabla, dilruba and vocals. Initially, he accompanied his father and older brother for Kirtan programs. This grooming led him to form his own ‘jatha’ in 1996, and he has been appointed as Hazoori Raagi in Sachkhand Sri Hazoor Sahib since then. He has also performed Kirtan across India in various states and cities. His first tour abroad was to Bangkok, Thailand in 2004. Since then, he has also visited Melbourne, Australia in 2017 and the United Kingdom in 2020. His Kirtan has been widely appreciated by the Sangat worldwide, especially for his renditions of the ‘Baani’ of Guru Gobind Singh Ji. His son, Satpratap Singh is currently following in his footsteps and has been learning from home. In addition to that, he has also come under the guidance of Pandit Shyamrao Gunjkar. Satpratap Singh now accompanies Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji on tabla and sometimes on the dilruba as well. He is also blessed with a melodious voice and provides beautiful ‘Alaaps’ during the Kirtan.
If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Gurpartap Singh Ji or his father, please contact us.
